meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Scale Your AI Projects
content.seo_description: Stop building apps in the dark. Learn to implement professional analytics with Antigravity and Claude Code to track user behavior and scale your portfolio.
content.answer_block: Most student projects fail because they lack visibility. By integrating lightweight, event-driven analytics, you transform a 'static' project into a data-backed product. Using Antigravity to instrument your codebase and Claude Code to automate the integration, you gain the precise insights needed to iterate faster and impress future employers.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks grinding out code, battling bugs, and finally pushing your app to production. But then, silence. You have no idea if anyone is using it, where they drop off, or what features they actually value. You are stuck in the 'basic app' purgatory because you are building in the dark, disconnected from the reality of user behavior.

Professional engineering is not just about writing code; it is about measuring impact. By implementing a robust analytics layer, you shift from being a coder to a product builder. This tutorial will show you how to leverage AI-assisted development tools to instrument your stack in minutes, turning your student project into a data-driven portfolio piece.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the three key actions that define a successful user journey. Avoid tracking everything; focus on 'Sign-ups', 'Feature Usage', and 'Conversion'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and suggest the three most critical user events I should track to understand engagement for a SaaS MVP.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review my project structure and list the 3 most important user interaction points for analytics tracking.'

steps.title: Initialize the Analytics Provider
steps.description: Set up a lightweight tracking client. We will use a provider that integrates seamlessly with your existing frontend framework.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, reusable analytics service wrapper that supports event tracking and user identification.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a new file analytics.ts and implement a singleton service for event tracking.'

steps.title: Instrument Critical User Flows
steps.description: Inject tracking calls into your main components. Focus on high-value interactions like button clicks and form submissions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Find all primary call-to-action buttons in my React components and wrap them with the tracking service I just created.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Search for all primary buttons and inject tracking events into their onClick handlers.'

steps.title: Automate User Context Tracking
steps.description: Ensure every event carries metadata like user IDs and session timestamps, which are vital for debugging and cohort analysis.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Modify the analytics service to automatically append user session metadata and current route info to every event track call.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Update the analytics service to include global user context in every event payload.'

steps.title: Validate and Deploy
steps.description: Verify your events are firing correctly in the network tab before pushing to production to ensure your data pipeline is clean.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a small test suite to verify that my analytics events fire correctly when a user clicks the signup button.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a test file to mock and verify analytics event dispatching.'

pro_tips: Always track 'error' events with stack traces; this turns your analytics dashboard into an automated bug-reporting tool.
Avoid the 'everything is an event' trap; prioritize tracking the 'Aha!' moment where the user derives actual value from your app.
Use distinct naming conventions like 'user_signed_up' vs 'button_clicked' to keep your database clean and queryable.
Implement an 'opt-out' toggle early to ensure your app follows basic privacy and compliance standards for your portfolio.
Use your analytics dashboard to create a 'User Journey Map' screenshot for your GitHub README; it proves you think like a Senior Engineer.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, wait 48 hours for data to populate, then create a dashboard report identifying your app's biggest bottleneck for user retention.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start engineering for impact. You have the tools, the strategy, and the roadmap to turn your project into a professional-grade application. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
